criteria.name: Problem Identification
criteria.description: Clearly identifies the key components of the ill-defined problem related to model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all relevant aspects of the problem, including the types of models involved and evaluation metrics.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to identify key components or misinterprets the problem context.

criteria.name: Task Specification
criteria.description: Translates the ill-defined problem into a well-defined task with clear objectives and constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Defines specific, measurable objectives and constraints that guide the model evaluation process.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides vague objectives or lacks clarity in task definition.

criteria.name: Contextual Relevance
criteria.description: Ensures that the defined task is relevant to current best practices in model evaluation and overfitting avoidance.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Aligns the task with established methodologies and contemporary challenges in machine learning.
criteria.poor_indicator: Ignores established practices or suggests irrelevant approaches.

typical_errors: Overlooking key aspects of model evaluation such as bias-variance tradeoff.
Failing to specify clear objectives, resulting in ambiguous tasks.
Misunderstanding the relevance of overfitting in the context of the problem.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Effectively identifies errors and gaps in the AI-generated output related to model evaluation and overfitting.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Clearly articulates all major errors and gaps, demonstrating a deep understanding of evaluation metrics and overfitting.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misses critical errors or identifies irrelevant issues unrelated to model evaluation.

criteria.name: Use of Evaluation Metrics
criteria.description: Evaluates the AI output using relevant evaluation metrics (e.g., accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score).
criteria.excellent_indicator: Selects and applies appropriate metrics for the context, demonstrating a solid understanding of their implications.
criteria.poor_indicator: Uses inappropriate or irrelevant metrics that do not align with the task.

criteria.name: Critical Analysis
criteria.description: Analyzes the AI output critically, considering the implications of identified errors on model performance.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides a thorough analysis of how errors affect overall model effectiveness and potential improvements.
criteria.poor_indicator: Offers superficial analysis without connecting errors to model performance.

typical_errors: Failing to recognize overfitting signs in the AI output.
Inadequate explanation of why certain evaluation metrics are more relevant than others.
Overlooking the importance of context when analyzing errors.

criteria.name: Correction Clarity
criteria.description: Provides clear and actionable correction commands to the AI based on identified errors.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Articulates precise and well-structured commands that directly address the errors identified.
criteria.poor_indicator: Issues vague or confusing commands that do not effectively guide the AI's corrections.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Demonstrates an understanding of iterative improvement by refining AI outputs through multiple correction cycles.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Successfully refines the AI output through thoughtful iterations that progressively enhance model evaluation.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to iterate effectively, resulting in minimal improvement or repeated errors.

criteria.name: Feedback Relevance
criteria.description: Gives relevant feedback that helps improve the AI's understanding and output regarding model evaluation.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Feedback is specific, relevant, and directly tied to the identified issues in the AI's response.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides irrelevant feedback that does not contribute to understanding or correcting the AI's output.

typical_errors: Offering corrections that do not align with the identified errors in the AI output.
Neglecting to iterate on corrections, leading to a stagnant output.
Providing feedback that is too general or unrelated to the specific issues at hand.
